reasoning: DRAM Bandwidth Utilization is 88.9% of peak, which is very high and indicates the kernel is saturating memory bandwidth. The arithmetic intensity is essentially 0.00 FLOP/byte, far below the ridge point of 62 FLOP/byte, confirming this is a memory-bound workload with minimal computation relative to data movement. While Long Scoreboard stalls are high at 55.6%, this is consistent with memory-bound behavior where warps are waiting on memory requests that are actively saturating DRAM bandwidth — the high DRAM utilization distinguishes this from a latency-bound scenario.
